title: Green Bean Bredie  –  just right for the cold
excerpt: Bredies are winter food, but not in our house. We eat bredies all year round. We alternate the meat between lamb and beef. Bredies are family food and we've always called it "spoon food" because you can eat it literally with a spoon. NEEDED: 1.5 kg stewing meat lamb or beef …

seo.title: Green Bean Bredie - just right for the cold - Marinda Kook

html:
Bredies are winter food, but not in our house. We eat bredies all year round. We alternate the meat between lamb and beef. Bredies are family food and we've always called it “spoon food” because you can eat it literally with a spoon.

NEEDED:

  • 1.5 kg stewing meat lamb or beef ( I used brisket)
  • 40 ml oil
  • 2 onions
  • 1 tin tomato mixture
  • 10 ml jeera (optional)
  • 5 cloves
  • 250 ml white wine
  • 300 ml warm water
  • 100 ml Worcester sauce
  • 60 ml vinegar
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • 3 potatoes cut into cubes
  • 1 kg green beans
  • a little nutmeg

NOW:

  1. slice the onions into rings and fry in the oil until glossy
  2. fry the jeera together with the onions
  3. cut the meat into smaller cubes and fry together with the onions
  4. now add the wine, water, tomato mixture, vinegar, Worcester sauce and cloves
  5. let it cook for around 60 minutes – if your sauce runs low, add more water.
  6. now add the potatoes, cut into quarters, and cook for another 20 minutes
  7. last, add the sliced beans, salt and pepper.
  8. now cook until everything is soft, but not mushy
  9. serve on rice and feast on the meal
